summary: Comprehensive testing of GST Invoice Management System after MongoDB migration. All 27 backend API tests pass (100%). Frontend UI testing completed successfully for all admin pages including Dashboard, Customers, Products, Expenses, Settings, Ledger, and Invoice creation. Fixed MongoDB ObjectId serialization bug in invoice creation endpoint.

fixed_during_testing.issue: MongoDB ObjectId serialization error - customer object contained _id field causing PydanticSerializationError
fixed_during_testing.fix: Added {'_id': 0} projection to customer and business queries in create_invoice endpoint

frontend: Login page loads correctly
Login with valid credentials navigates to dashboard
Dashboard displays stats cards (Total Invoices, Revenue, Customers, Products)
Dashboard shows Net Profit, Total Expenses, Pending Payments
Dashboard shows recent invoices table
Customers page loads and displays customer cards
Add Customer modal opens and form works
Customer creation with success toast
Products page loads and displays product cards
Add Product modal opens and form works
Product creation with success toast
Expenses page loads and displays expense table
Add Expense modal opens and form works
Expense creation with success toast
Settings page loads with business form
Ledger page loads with financial summary
Invoices page loads with invoice list
Create Invoice page loads
Customer selection shows GST type indicator
Navigation between all admin pages works

critical_code_review_comments: MongoDB ObjectId serialization was missing in create_invoice endpoint - FIXED
All other endpoints properly exclude _id field with {'_id': 0} projection
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py - Fixed MongoDB ObjectId serialization in create_invoice endpoint (line 534, 538)
seed_data_creation: Test data created with TEST_ prefix for customers, products, expenses, and invoices during API testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend APIs and frontend pages are working correctly after MongoDB migration. The invoice creation flow properly calculates CGST/SGST for intra-state and IGST for inter-state transactions. Test data with TEST_ prefix exists in the database. Authentication uses JWT tokens stored in localStorage.
